topic: Quality of earnings and margin bridge review

deck.title: Quality of earnings and margin bridge review

sections.title: Quality Of Earnings Overview
sections.decision_purpose: Confirm normalized earnings base for valuation.

sections.title: P&L Walkthrough
sections.decision_purpose: Identify repeatable vs non-recurring margin drivers.

sections.title: Margin Bridge
sections.decision_purpose: Quantify bridge from FY22 baseline to TTM margin.

assumptions: Management accounts are normalized to CAD thousands.
FY22-FY23 and TTM Jun-24 use the same chart of accounts for trend comparability.

sections.title: Quality Of Earnings Overview
sections.content:
Normalized EBITDA rose from 16.6M in FY22 to 22.0M TTM Jun-24, but valuation sensitivity depends on sustainability of pro forma adjustments.
- Reported EBITDA increased from 13.9M in FY22 to 22.3M TTM Jun-24.
- Due diligence adjustments moved from +1.3M in FY22 to -0.6M TTM, indicating quality deterioration in recent period.
- Pro forma adjustments shifted from +1.7M FY22 to -0.3M TTM as temporary supports rolled off.
- Pro forma EBITDA margin still expanded from 28.7% to 31.2%, supporting baseline multiple defense.
- Strategic implication: Valuation case should anchor on pro forma run-rate but haircut adjustments with weak persistence evidence.
- Strategic implication: IC memo should separate structural cost normalization from discretionary one-time add-backs.
- Decision required: Approve valuation range using normalized EBITDA with downside case for adjustment durability.

authoring_payload.headline_claim: Normalized EBITDA rose from 16.6M in FY22 to 22.0M TTM Jun-24, but valuation sensitivity depends on sustainability of pro forma adjustments.
claims: Reported EBITDA increased from 13.9M in FY22 to 22.3M TTM Jun-24.
Due diligence adjustments moved from +1.3M in FY22 to -0.6M TTM, indicating quality deterioration in recent period.
Pro forma adjustments shifted from +1.7M FY22 to -0.3M TTM as temporary supports rolled off.
Pro forma EBITDA margin still expanded from 28.7% to 31.2%, supporting baseline multiple defense.
implications: Valuation case should anchor on pro forma run-rate but haircut adjustments with weak persistence evidence.
IC memo should separate structural cost normalization from discretionary one-time add-backs.
authoring_payload.decision_ask: Approve valuation range using normalized EBITDA with downside case for adjustment durability.

sections.title: P&L Walkthrough
sections.content:
Gross profit growth is durable, but margin expansion depends on keeping pass-through and productivity gains ahead of cost inflation.
- Revenue grew from 512 in FY22 to 624 in TTM Jun-24 (+22%), led by transaction and card-management volumes.
- Gross profit increased from 392 to 491 (+25%), outpacing revenue and indicating improved mix.
- EBITDA rose from 118 to 161 (+36%), with margin expansion from 23.0% to 25.8%.
- Operating leverage remains sensitive to contract services and integration labor absorption.
- Strategic implication: Underwriting case should assume moderate operating leverage, not full straight-line margin expansion.
- Strategic implication: Post-close value creation plan should target contract-services productivity and integration automation.
- Decision required: Adopt base-case margin trajectory with explicit sensitivity to contract-services cost inflation.

authoring_payload.headline_claim: Gross profit growth is durable, but margin expansion depends on keeping pass-through and productivity gains ahead of cost inflation.
claims: Revenue grew from 512 in FY22 to 624 in TTM Jun-24 (+22%), led by transaction and card-management volumes.
Gross profit increased from 392 to 491 (+25%), outpacing revenue and indicating improved mix.
EBITDA rose from 118 to 161 (+36%), with margin expansion from 23.0% to 25.8%.
Operating leverage remains sensitive to contract services and integration labor absorption.
implications: Underwriting case should assume moderate operating leverage, not full straight-line margin expansion.
Post-close value creation plan should target contract-services productivity and integration automation.
authoring_payload.decision_ask: Adopt base-case margin trajectory with explicit sensitivity to contract-services cost inflation.

sections.title: Margin Bridge
sections.content:
TTM margin expansion is primarily mix and productivity led, partially offset by inflation and support-cost drag.
- Baseline margin in FY22 was 23.0%.
- Revenue growth contribution added +2.8 percentage points to margin trajectory.
- Pass-through recovery and pricing/mix contributed +2.0 points combined.
- Inflation and support drag reduced margin by -0.6 points before landing at 25.8% net margin.
- Strategic implication: Margin underwriting should separate structural productivity gains from cyclical pass-through recovery.
- Strategic implication: Value plan should lock in productivity programs before assuming additional pricing/mix uplift.
- Decision required: Approve margin case with explicit downside scenario at 24.6% if cost drag persists.
